stem: A ski gondola cable stretches 60 feet from one support tower to the next, making a 37-degree angle with the horizontal. Given that sin 37° ≈ 3/5, cos 37° ≈ 4/5, and tan 37° ≈ 3/4, what is the horizontal distance between the two towers, in feet?
explanation: The cable is the hypotenuse (60 feet) and the horizontal distance is the side adjacent to the 37° angle. Using cos 37° = adjacent/hypotenuse: horizontal distance = 60 × cos 37° = 60 × (4/5) = 240/5 = 48 feet.

distractorRationale.A: Used sin instead of cos, finding the vertical rise instead of the horizontal distance: 60 × (3/5) = 36.
distractorRationale.B: Set up the cosine ratio inverted, dividing by cos instead of multiplying: 60 ÷ (4/5) = 60 × (5/4) = 75.
distractorRationale.C: Used tan instead of cos, applying the wrong trig ratio: 60 × (3/4) = 45.

imageDescription: A right triangle showing a ski gondola cable as the hypotenuse labeled 60 ft, stretching diagonally between two support towers. The angle between the cable and the horizontal ground is labeled 37°. The horizontal distance between the towers is marked with a question mark.
